issues.description: Script uses GM_xmlhttpRequest to communicate with SponsorBlock API and multiple Cobalt instances for video download. These are third-party servers and may receive YouTube video IDs and possibly other metadata.
issues.recommendation: Ensure only minimal, non-sensitive data is sent. Review SponsorBlock and Cobalt privacy policies. Warn users about potential privacy implications.

issues.description: Script stores user toggle settings and state using GM_setValue/GM_getValue, but does not appear to collect or transmit sensitive user data (e.g., cookies, form values, clipboard, or keyboard input).
issues.recommendation: Continue to avoid collecting sensitive data. Periodically review for privacy leaks.

issues.description: No evidence of eval, new Function, setTimeout(string), setInterval(string), or dynamic script injection. No @require or document.write usage.
issues.recommendation: Maintain strict avoidance of remote code execution patterns.

issues.description: No code obfuscation detected. Script is readable, not minified or obfuscated.
No DOM XSS risk detected. User input and URL parameters are not directly inserted into innerHTML/outerHTML.
issues.recommendation: Continue to sanitize any future user input handling.

issues.description: Script requests GM_xmlhttpRequest, GM_setValue, GM_getValue, GM_addStyle. All are used in code. No evidence of unused high-risk grants.
Script connects to multiple third-party APIs (SponsorBlock, Cobalt instances). These are not official YouTube APIs and pose supply chain risk if endpoints are compromised.
issues.recommendation: Prefer official APIs or well-known, audited endpoints. Monitor for endpoint changes or compromise.

details: Stores toggle settings and state via GM_setValue/GM_getValue
Does not collect cookies, form values, clipboard, or keyboard input

script_name: EasyTube V3 — Ad Skip, SponsorBlock & HD Download⬇️🚀
summary: Script transmits YouTube video IDs and possibly metadata to third-party APIs (SponsorBlock and Cobalt instances) for ad skipping and video downloading. No evidence of sensitive data collection or code execution risks. Supply chain risk exists due to reliance on non-official endpoints. Overall, script is readable and permissions are appropriate, but users should be aware of privacy implications and supply chain risks.
